scope.question: extraction_refresh: DKD, IPF, and AD-risk human studies now have retained PubMed source snapshots supporting abstract-level sample, dosing, timepoint, effect, and safety details, and the legacy IPF generic feasibility/tolerability extraction has been reconciled to the N=12 randomized pilot. Full article/table extraction for DKD, IPF, AD-risk, and D+Q bone supplements remains incomplete. Suggested action: Retain or ingest full-text/table source snapshots for DKD, IPF, AD-risk, and D+Q bone supplements, then reconcile abstract-level extraction against article tables before formal synthesis.

outputs.summary: Created or retained the repair candidate and updated the senolytics coverage assessment. The assessment now distinguishes metadata-only PubMed EFetch abstract extraction for DKD, IPF, and AD-risk from retained registry/PMC full-text support for D+Q bone, and keeps full article/table and supplement extraction debt open. No evidence_review lane records were created because the candidate lifecycle_status is submitted, not in_review.

proposed_records.rationale: Tracks this bounded extraction-refresh repair candidate and its required review lanes.

Clarifies that the DKD, IPF, and AD-risk PubMed EFetch snapshots are metadata-only abstract sources, preserves the D+Q retained registry/PMC article support, and keeps full article/table and supplement extraction debt explicit.

quality_checks.summary: Read the context pack first and kept inspection bounded to named schemas, target records, source snapshots, text snapshots, and scoped artifacts.

Confirmed DKD, IPF, and AD-risk PubMed snapshots are metadata-only; NCT04313634 registry and PMID 38956196 PMC author-manuscript artifacts are retained; the D+Q bone supplement PDF is linked but not normalized into retained tables.

Candidate proposed_records lists the candidate_change create and coverage_assessment update; required_review_lanes exactly match extraction_fidelity, safety_limitations, synthesis_boundary, and taxonomy_mapping.

blocking_issues: PMID 31542391, PMID 36857968, and PMID 40010154 are retained only as metadata-only PubMed EFetch snapshots, so DKD, IPF, and AD-risk cannot be upgraded beyond abstract-level extraction or reconciled against article tables in this bounded pass.
The retained PMID 38956196 PMC article identifies a supplement PDF link, but supplement tables are not retained as normalized source artifacts, so D+Q bone supplement extraction remains incomplete.
next_actions: Retain or ingest full-text/table source snapshots for DKD, IPF, and AD-risk, then reconcile abstract-level extraction against article tables before formal synthesis.
Retain and normalize D+Q bone supplement tables before extracting supplement safety or efficacy details.
Let coordinator post-run regenerate exports and run verify:knowledge-base after writing this agent_run output.
